original: English archaeologistHoward Carteropens Tutankhamun's nearly intact tomb in Egypt
NASA's InSight mission lands on Mars after a 7 month voyage
UK nonprofit Raspberry Pi releases its $5 Zero computer, sells out in a day
In Basse-Terre, Guadeloupe, 1.5 inches (38.1 mm) of rain falls in a single minute, the heaviest recorded rainfall in history
1stPolaroidcamera sold for $89.75 in Boston at the Jordan Marsh department store. The Land Camera model 95 becomes prototype for all Polaroid Land cameras for next 15 years
"Alice in Wonderland" byLewis Carrollis published in America
Third of a species begin dying (23,000) over a two day period, the spectacled flying foxes, unable to survive heatwave of 42 degrees in northern Queensland, Australia
The first lion exhibited in America is displayed in Boston
British explorer CaptainJames Cookis the first European to visit Maui in the Sandwich Islands (now Hawaii)
France launches 1st satellite, 92 lb (42 kg) A1-capsule (Astérix), named after the popular French comic character

USSR coxless pairs scull rower Viktor Ivanov wins Olympic silver medal; in his excitement he drops his medal, it sinks in Lake Wendouree (Victoria, Australia) and is later recovered by a local teenager
Official opening of Thomas Telford's Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, carrying the Llangollen canal 126 feet above the River Dee. Longest aqueduct in the UK and highest canal aqueduct in the world.
1st major tidal power plant opens at Rance estuary, France
Supersonic airplane the Concorde makes its last ever flight, returning to Bristol, England.
WHO labels COVID-19 variant Omicron a "variant of concern", with its dozens of new mutations, after it emerges in Botswana and South Africa
(Marshall) "Major" Taylor, American track cyclist (first African-American world champion; 1899 World Track C'ships), born in Indianapolis, Indiana (d. 1932)
Mary Edwards Walker, American surgeon and women's rights leader and only woman to receive Medal of Honor (bravery during Civil War), born in Oswego, New York (d. 1919)
Japanese naval carrier force left its base & moves east toward Pearl Harbor
NHL forms with Montreal Canadiens, Montreal Maroons, Toronto Arenas, Ottawa Senators & Quebec Bulldogs; National Hockey Association disbands
Amos Alonzo Stagg of University of Chicago creates American football huddle
1st large indoor football game, University of Chicago beats University of Michigan 7-6
1st 3D feature film "Bwana Devil" directed by Arch Oboler premieres in Los Angeles, advertised as "The Miracle of the Age!!! A LION in your lap! A LOVER in your arms!"
Refrigerated railroad car patented by JB Sutherland of Detroit
First multidrug randomized control trial to treat Ebola started in Democratic Republic of Congo announced by the WHO
Australian sprinterBetty Cuthbertwins the coveted 100m gold medal in 11.5 at the Melbourne Olympics; takes sprint double winning 200m final four days later
Dutch driverMax Verstappenconfirms his third consecutive world F1 drivers championship with his record 19th GP win of the season in Abu Dhabi; his Red Bull team retains constructors title
NFL NY Giant placekicker Péte Gogolák scores team record 8 PAT (points after touchdown) in 62-10 win over Philadelphia Eagles at Yankee Stadium, NYC
American weightlifter Charles Vinci wins bantamweight gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics with world-record 3-lift (snatch, clean & jerk, overhead press) total of 342.5 kilograms
Twins John Castino & Jays Alfredo Griffin tie for AL Rookie of Year

British Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonfinishes 2nd in season-ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix at Yas Marina Circuit to claim his 4th F1 World Drivers Championship; wins title by 46 points fromSebastian Vettel
DC superhero film "Aquaman" directed by James Wan starringJason MomoaandAmber Heardpremieres in London
Ernie Coombs, American-Canadian children's entertainer (Mr. Dressup), born in Lewiston, Maine (d. 2001)
The Beatles re-record "Please Please Me" at a faster tempo, based on producerGeorge Martin's suggestion
The Beatles record "Ask Me Why" at EMI Studio, London
Rock band Cream gives "final" concert at Royal Albert Hall, London, England - but re-unites briefly for a short tour in 2005
Charlie "Bird" Parkerleads a recording session with Dizzy Gillespie andMiles Davisfor the Savoy label, marketed as the "greatest jazz session ever"
First national Thanksgiving in America
West Virginia created as a result of dispute over slavery with Virginia
SS Portland “The Titanic of New England” leaves for Cape Cod, shipwrecked off Cape Ann, all 192 on board killed
HMS Beagle leaves Tahiti for New Zealand
The last international rugby match before demolition of Dublin's famous Lansdowne Road Stadium is Ireland's 61-17 win over the Pacific Islanders
Greenidge scores 107 in 2nd innings of Test Cricket debut v India
Test Cricket debut ofColin Cowdreyv Australia at Gabba
23rd NASA Space Shuttle Mission (61-B): Atlantis 2 launches
Columbia mated to SRBs & external tank at Vehicle Assembly Building
Alexander Volkov, Sergei Krikalev & Jean-Loup Chretien launch
Explorer 18 spacecraft launched by NASA
Pioneer 6's closest approach to Earth since 1965 launch (1.87 M km)
Minneapolis-St Paul baseball club takes the name Twins
TV game show "The Price Is Right", hosted by Bill Cullen, debuts on NBC
First streetcar railway in America starts operating in New York City with a 12-cent fare
First successful Technicolor movie "The Toll of the Sea" premieres at the Rialto Theatre in NYC
Turkish parliament bestows President Mustafa Kemal with the surname Atatürk, meaning 'Father of the Turks' as part of the country's modernization effort
British geneticist Alec Jeffreys who developed widely-used techniques for DNA fingerprinting, becomes honorary freeman of the city of Leicester
Lebanon independence first proclaimed by France
The University of Notre Dame is founded
Go-Daigo, 96th Emperor of Japan (1318-39) who successfully overthrew the Kamakura shogunate, establishing the Kenmu Restoration (1333-36), born in Heian Kyō, Japan (d. 1339)
Bat Masterson, American gunfighter in the Wild West, born in Henriville, Canada East (d. 1921)
Maurice "Mac" McDonald, American fast food pioneer, co-founder of McDonald's, born in Manchester, New Hampshire (d. 1971)
Tracy Hickman, American sci-fi author (Dragons of Spring Dawning), born in Salt Lake City, Utah
